$comment: forge↔project contract config for mdtoc, forge's creds-free OOTB reference project. Exercises every contract component: Studio fields (northStar/instructions/demoProcess/skills/kb), a fast deterministic quality gate, a creds-free acceptance gate against a fixture, a bound project skill, a Brain 3 under forge/, and a full releaseProcess (in-cycle docs+changelog drafts + a pre-merge version bump). The quality gate is the OFFLINE unit suite (npm test → node:test, <1s); the acceptance gate runs the BUILT CLI against test/fixtures/release-notes.md and reads back the exact TOC.
$acceptance_gate_comment: Creds-free acceptance tier (contract C7). `match` identifies the live-vs-real gate by the `acceptance` token in a WI's quality_gate_cmd (`npm run acceptance`). required:true forces the PM to emit ≥1 WI whose gate runs the acceptance suite, so a change to TOC behaviour is always proven against the BUILT CLI, not just units. requires_env is empty: this project is creds-free, so the acceptance gate runs with no env preconditions.
$standing_work_item_acs_comment: Static testing invariants forge appends to EVERY work item (contract A2/C7/C9). Encodes the read-back + non-default-fixture discipline as standing ACs.
$releaseProcess_comment: Release contract C10. In-cycle: keep docs (README/roadmap) current and draft a `## [Unreleased]` CHANGELOG entry. Pre-merge: the release-finalizer bumps the version in package.json + promotes the draft. Tag + GitHub release are CI's job (.github/workflows/release.yml, installed from studio/starters).
name: mdtoc
northStar: A tiny, dependency-free CLI that turns a Markdown doc's headings into a correct, GitHub-compatible table of contents.
instructions: Keep runtime dependencies at zero — node builtins only; tsx/typescript are the sole dev deps. Make the implementation satisfy the tests as written; never edit a test to make it pass. Keep source files small and feature-organised (immutable returns, validate argv at the boundary). Acceptance fixtures must use non-default sentinel values asserted via a read-back of the built CLI's output. Record each initiative's plan + demo under forge/history/<initiative-id>/ so the repo carries a browsable build record; project skills live under .forge/skills/.
standing_work_item_acs: Acceptance read-back: the change is exercised by `npm run acceptance`, which runs the BUILT CLI (`dist/cli.js`) against `test/fixtures/release-notes.md` and asserts the exact generated TOC. The WI that proves a TOC-behaviour change MUST set its quality_gate_cmd to the acceptance command and update the EXPECTED constant in test/acceptance/run.ts in lock-step.

Non-default fixtures (C9): any heading under test in the fixture carries a distinctive, non-default value (e.g. a `sentinel-<hex>` section) and duplicate/edge cases (a repeated heading, a fenced code block) so a TOC that ignores the real heading text is caught by the read-back.
Quality gate stays fast + green: `npm test` (node:test) is deterministic, creds-free, and sub-second; it must fail before the work exists and pass only when the behaviour is correct.
Docs + changelog current: a TOC-behaviour change is not done until README/docs describe it and a `## [Unreleased]` CHANGELOG.md entry is drafted (the release-finalizer promotes it at pre-merge).

demoProcess.text: Run `npm run demo` to execute the built mdtoc CLI against test/fixtures/release-notes.md and capture the real generated table of contents (the same path the acceptance suite proves).

Read back the captured TOC against the expected output — the sentinel section `Quickstart sentinel-7f3a9c`, the duplicate-anchor `#rotate-the-signing-key-1`, and the absence of the fenced `## Fake Heading` must all hold.
Attach forge/history/<initiative-id>/demo/toc-capture.md (command + captured TOC + read-back result) to the PR as the demo evidence.

steps.text: Update README.md and roadmap.md so the documented CLI surface matches the merged behaviour (flags, examples).
Draft a changelog entry under `## [Unreleased]` in CHANGELOG.md describing the change.
Bump the semantic version in package.json to match the promoted changelog heading.
